transcript.text: How are you?

It's been going.

And I also know I still owe you that document, which
Let me just check in with Sid, who's on product.
I feel like he might have added stuff.

No, and this is freaking annoying.
Okay.
Let me ping him and add Mark.
Okay, I will make sure to get this to you because I think he has, like, pulled, like, all the documents together, but, like, the product feature matrix is what I'm talking about.
Yep.
Okay.
So, I'm sure you have an agenda.
A lot of it was just following up on that chat we had earlier this week.
Yeah.

Yeah, because I don't want you.
on new content will allow us to get to all the revisions that we need to.
Yeah.
So, and we'll definitely prioritize the AI articles.
I think there's, let's see, about 23 of them.
Yeah.
Which is almost half of the outstanding ones.
Yeah.
So we'll definitely focus there.
I think Gabby's out this week for vacation anyway.
So it was good news.
She gets back on Monday and I'll tell her that we have some updates to make.
But I think we'll be able to kind of get through the AI stuff fairly quickly.
And then hopefully we'll have somebody on the team ready to kind of just look through those kind of product mentions.
Yeah.
Okay.
So think that works for us for the time being.
Okay.
Thank you.
And then I think like I also had a request for SEO, like just support in general.
you.
Oh, I don't know if you have an update there.
Yeah, I asked our VP, and he thinks we might be able to help with some of the on-page recommendations that we did.

But for ongoing SEO, I think that's where I just wanted to get a little bit more clarity, because I think we can definitely help from the technical side, because I think most of the stuff that we pointed out in the audit isn't such a big lift.
Yeah.
Um, but wondering just kind of at a broad stroke, like, what kind of support you guys are looking for ongoing?
Um, I think, like, to be honest, we don't have anyone who specializes in SEO.
Like, we just don't have anyone who, like, knows their way around it.
Um, we don't even have someone who has, like, dangerous half knowledge.
So I think, like, we need to have someone who we can consult whenever, like,

First of all, like cleaning up our landing pages and like our website and making it into like a demand gen channel, so to say, because like right now everyone's just publishing the way they want to and it's all over the place.
There's no strategy.
I mean, also like the technical stuff, like we constantly, I think we have like 5,000 errors like on our HubSpot that says like you need to clean up your SEO, like so that.
And then thirdly, like once we have everything in place, just someone who keeps us honest and is like, hey, like we need to focus on this area.
We need to focus on that area.

So I think it's just, I mean, I don't know if that answers your question, but like just someone who is like a consultant and can help us just get our website in an SEO optimized state so that it like starts pulling weight and like lift because our website right now is not really doing anything for us.
Yeah, think in the.
That makes sense.

See if he can get somebody on his team to kind of go in and, like, not just make the recommendations or kind of follow the recommendations that we had already given, but, like, see if we can mock it up to kind of help you guys visualize what it would look like.
Yeah.
So I'll put a ticket in to get one of our directors to lend out for now and then see if there's anybody in our network that we might know that can kind of be more of that consultant ongoing.
Okay.
Thank you.
Absolutely.
Very much appreciated.
Yeah.
No, I get the struggle.
It's, like, if nobody in-house owns it, then it No, nobody owns it.
Like, nobody has an idea.
Like, I mean, I have, like, I have the understanding that it's important, but, like, I wouldn't be able to, like, create a strategy and execute on it.
Yeah.
And then we have, like, folks on the team who are like, oh, that's overrated.
We don't need it.
And I'm like, you guys.
The we are producing in publishing seems to be, like, with these early signals, pretty effective.
So you can only imagine once we unlock the backlog of, like, 48 articles, we can start to see that traffic continue to kind of compound and go up.
Yes.
To the right.
Yes, for sure.
And, I mean, we're all aligned.
The problem is just that, like, we're so under-researched, like, so understaffed.
It's insane.
So, but again, like, I'm just, all my hopes are in the product marketer.
I'm going to push Sid to, like, who's our product owner for AI and agents to just, like, go over this feature matrix today.
I'm going to say, if you don't do today, I've, and then I'll send it to you.
And then, like, hopefully we can just, like, move forward with the AI articles and then we'll be fine.
Yeah.
Figure it out.
Awesome.
Okay.
Okay.
Thank you.
Couple Couple questions.

And one out of coming out of the kind of planning session you guys had a couple weeks ago, is there any like larger campaigns or focus areas of kind of what you guys are looking to do in 2026 that might be relevant to kind of what we're doing?
Yeah.
So I think like we're going to talk about and how like, so it's a multiple things.
So like agents and how they like support and how they differentiate between like add-on AI versus like AI that is in the actual engagement.
Secondly, like what's super important and that's like feedback that we've gotten from a lot of customers is that there's a lot of fear around AI and like AI taking over and like replacing the human.
However, the way Fieldguide operates.
and works is that AI supports the human and, like, makes work easier for the human.
The judgment piece still stays on the human, so to say.
And then I think, like, thirdly, we just kind of want to position ourselves as, like, the leader in the market.
And I've been saying, like, when, if we want to do that, we need to also, like, start with these competitive articles.
So I've sent that to Mark.
I'm actually going to bump this up again with him.
And he said he will get back to me on, like, a strategy.
I think he needs to just read through it this week, and he said, and then he can get back to me.
Because that's a way to, like, position ourselves as the leader and, like, own the narrative.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah, it's a tactic that's working really well across a lot of our clients.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Especially like B2B SaaS, that's most buyers now, like at least 60% are starting their searches with some kind of AI overview or within ChatGPT or Claude or perplexity.

And so luckily, like if people have the right, you know, industry kind of point of view and knowledge, if they search for like what are the best audit advisory platforms with AI, like Fieldguide is showing up across multiple platforms.

I think if they don't have that specific query and it's just like what are the best or most modern audit and advisory platforms without mentioning AI or agents, that's when it kind of defaults back to the legacy players and like some of the smaller like data snipper and others.
So that's where like we want to start to put out content that helps.
Fieldguide boosts entity recognition of like, oh, Fieldguide is in this space, and they are modern, and they have AI and agents.
So a lot of kind of the proposed topics that we have in there are focused on doing that specifically.
And then we can get a lot of different kind of cuts of how we position comparison content.
So outside of, I think we have like eight right now, we could definitely expand.
Like one of my other clients is Navan, and we're doing probably 20 to 30 comparison articles for them.
So just food for thought.
I know we've already got the POV for Mark, but I think it really will move the needle, both for like LLM visibility and then also search visibility.
Yeah.
Okay, that makes sense.
Um, my other question was, like, what would we say our North Star metric would be from our content?
Is it really?
Driving Demos, kind of getting people to click on, like scheduling time with your team and sales team to have a conversation or what else might we track?
I mean, I would say like, yeah, I mean, demos, but I think like, I think like what I would love to see is like, and I don't know if that's possible.

But like, if they come to our site, like reading these articles, and then like click and continue like consuming content on our website, like downloading some of the white papers that we have, or just like, you know, like, because I feel like the way it works is like we're sparking their interest.
But what we want to achieve is like that they become like a qualified kind of MQL.
And that's like...

The first touchpoint in the journey, so to say, that's what, because like, I don't think like, people will immediately book a demo after like, they go to like a GrowthX article, like so top of funnel, like, there needs to be kind of a journey across the board.

So like, I don't know, they click an article, maybe we can have like links within the article to like other white papers, or like other pieces of content that we have, maybe like a webinar, maybe like some of the product videos that we have.
And then like, from there, they become an MQL, and we continue to nurture them.
And then like, eventually, they become pipeline.
But that's how I view it more.
Like, I, I don't, I think it's too like, up funnel to like, be able to expect that people will immediately like book a demo.
Yeah.
Yeah, I definitely agree.
Because I assume you're going to sales cycles much longer than.
You know, a few months, probably 18 months or so.
It depends.
It can be like three to, I would say, eight.
But yeah, for sure.
And I think it's also like such a, it's like such a complex tool to learn and to understand and comprehend.
Because like all of our competitors are selling AI for audit, AI for advisory, but like their AI is very different to what like it is that we're offering.
Essentially, like with our tool, you don't need to have any of the point solutions anymore in place that you do need with the other competitors.
And everything lives in one platform.
And it's actually very interesting how like people see videos of our product and how it works.
And they're just like, they don't believe it.
They're like, oh my gosh, like, are you, is this for real?
Um, and you
Usually like our product videos, our demos, they're usually the ones that like spark a lot of interest.
So I would say like driving them to understand the product more, driving them to understand like the vision of our company more.
Because like essentially what we're saying is like if you use our tool, you can free up so much like busy work that you're focused on.

That your role will change and you will become like a person in the corporation who can focus more on strategy, who can focus more on future vision and like do more things that actually move the business forward instead of like checking individual spreadsheets or like queuing images.
So yeah, I think that's how I see it more.
Okay.
So it, it.

Something based on what you said, like we could track in here, but again, that's harder to do with Google Analytics because it is last touch attribution, which means they would have to be on a download page to start that download.
Yes.

So, yeah, if there's a way to track, I guess, any maybe first touch attribution, that might be a way around it, but otherwise it's going to be hard for us to look at kind of, is the content working in the way that you want it to, at least from the kind of Google Analytics view that we have here.
Okay, that makes sense.
It's like, what about like referrals from like Allen Lenz?
Is that something we can track?
I mean, I see it in HubSpot.
I just don't know how accurate it is.
Yep.
So.
This is specific to just our content of like LOM referral traffic to the content itself.
All right.
I mean, that's already like a great thing to track, I think.
And then I think the second click would be like, is this qualified traffic?
But then- That's usually where we look at engagement rate.
It's usually much higher than organic traffic.
Right.
Okay.
Yeah, we will be reporting on this.
Once we have the articles up.
Yes, absolutely.
And hopefully we'll see kind of a much larger uptick.
Yeah.
Because like another client I'm working with is Redis, and they started about the same time as you guys.
And we're already seeing like 50 or so, just the last month's referrals.
Oh this makes me sad.
Okay.
Yeah.
They've put out, I think, 50 articles.
So we're clipping at a pretty good pace, but they were also in a backlog for up until this month.
We had about not quite as large as your backlog, but it was like 25 or 26 articles that we had to get through.
And they threw about four or five people at it.
Okay.
I mean, I feel like we're your worst client at the moment, but.
No, there's, I have one other that's a real pain that we've only got like six articles published in three and a half months.
Oh my gosh.
Okay.
Well, I hope I make up for it in charm.
Oh, absolutely.
Okay.
Thank you, Erik.
So I'm going to just bully Sid into like reviewing this product feature matrix.
And then I hope, I'm praying that I can get to you by end of day.
Okay.
And then we'll go from there.
Sounds like a plan.
Okay, thank you.
Enjoy your weekend.
Dina.
Yep, you too.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content strategy, SEO support, and unblocking the article backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=282.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content production is paused** pending the Product Feature Matrix, which is required to accurately revise the 48-article backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)
  - [**Top priority is revising \~23 AI articles** once the matrix is delivered; Gabby returns Monday to lead the effort.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)
  - [**GrowthX will provide immediate SEO support** (mockups, recommendations) and source a long-term consultant to fix the site's \~5,000 HubSpot errors and build a demand-gen strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=413.0)
  - [**The content North Star is driving MQLs** via multi-touch journeys, not direct demos. Key metrics will be LLM referral traffic and engagement.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1071.0)

## Topics

### Content Backlog & Blockers

  - [**Blocker:** The 48-article backlog is stalled, preventing new content production.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)
  - [**Cause:** Revisions require the Product Feature Matrix to ensure accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)
  - [**Priority:** Once unblocked, the team will focus on the \~23 AI articles first.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)
  - [**Owner:** Gabby (returns Monday) will lead the revisions.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)

### SEO Support Needs

  - [**Problem:** Fieldguide lacks in-house SEO expertise, resulting in a disorganized website and \~5,000 HubSpot errors.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0)
  - [**Goal:** Transform the website into a demand-gen channel with a clear strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=475.0)
  - [**GrowthX Support:**](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=413.0)
      - [**Immediate:** A director will create mockups and implement technical fixes from the audit.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [**Long-term:** Source a consultant for ongoing strategy and accountability.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=675.0)

### 2026 Content Strategy

  - [**Pillar 1: Agents & AI**](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=841.0)
      - [Differentiate Fieldguide's integrated AI from competitors' add-on solutions.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=857.0)
  - [**Pillar 2: Human-in-the-Loop**](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=882.0)
      - [Counter AI fear by showing it supports humans, freeing them for strategic work.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=882.0)
  - [**Pillar 3: Market Leadership**](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=913.0)
      - [Own the narrative with competitive comparison articles (e.g., 20–30 like Navan's) to boost LLM/search visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=913.0)

### Content Performance & Metrics

  - [**North Star:** Drive MQLs, not direct demos.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1071.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The product is complex, and sales cycles are long (3–8 months).](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1194.0)
      - [**Goal:** Move users from top-of-funnel articles to deeper content (whitepapers, videos) to qualify them.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1093.0)
  - [**Key Metrics:**](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1426.0)
      - [**LLM Referral Traffic:** Volume and engagement rate.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1426.0)
      - [**Engagement Rate:** A proxy for traffic quality.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1451.0)
  - [**Benchmark:** Redis (another GrowthX client) achieved \~50 LLM referrals last month after publishing \~50 articles, demonstrating the potential impact of clearing the backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1479.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Yasine:**](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1539.0)
      - [Secure the Product Feature Matrix from Sid today (Feb 20).](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=808.0)
      - [Send the matrix to Erik.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=1539.0)
  - [**Erik:**](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [Assign a director to begin SEO mockups and technical fixes.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [Source a long-term SEO consultant for Fieldguide.](https://fathom.video/share/HHV5LSq2D5GqcCEyXo8J-BPuoDe7gZfc?tab=summary&timestamp=675.0)

action_items.description: Pause new article production until AI feature matrix received
Brief Gabby on AI article updates on Feb 23
Create ticket for GrowthX director to assist Fieldguide SEO; mock on-page recs
Review AI feature matrix w/ Sid; send to Erik by EOD Feb 20
Bump Mark re: competitive content strategy
Report LOM referral traffic + engagement rate once articles live
